destinations.evidence: The payload builder converts DeliveryReady pool entries into prepared Phase 2 delivery settlement candidates.
The prepared transaction is an AsyncSettlement delivery call containing the final delivery result and delivery SPC call.
The async pool entry is removed only after a canonical Phase 2 delivery settlement is observed, not when the builder prepares the candidate.

equivalence_argument: DeliveryReady entries become Phase 2 settlement candidates during payload building and are removed only by canonical delivery settlement processing.
rationale: The payload builder adds a Phase 4.5 delivery phase, gets delivery-ready transactions from the pool, creates and executes Phase 2 settlement transactions, and explicitly defers pool removal to canonicalization for multi-validator correctness. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: During payload building, DeliveryReady long-running async jobs must be converted into Phase 2 async settlement transactions that include the delivery result and are executed without removing the async pool entry until canonicalization.
suggested_falsification_tests: Place a 0x0805 job in DeliveryReady with a delivery_result, build a payload, and assert the payload includes an AsyncSettlement carrying that delivery result while the async pool entry remains until the block is canonicalized.
summary: DeliveryReady jobs produce Phase 2 settlements and wait for canonical removal.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Place a DeliveryReady job in the pool, build a payload, and assert an AsyncSettlement deliver transaction is prepared while the pool entry remains until canonical delivery processing.
title: DeliveryReady jobs produce Phase 2 settlements
